action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|.........|
 2|.#p...S.#|
 3|#...#A#..|
 4|..#...B.x|
 5|.#.#..#..|
 6|...2.....|
 7|....##...|
 8|..q..1..#|
 9|..y......|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (7,2) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (7,2), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (6,3): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {0, 1, 2}. So it is open at t=0,1,2,6,7,8,... and closed at t=3,4,5,9,10,11,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (7,4): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {0, 1, 5}. So it is open at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... and closed at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (3,2); t mod 8=1: (4,2); t mod 8=2: (4,1); t mod 8=3: (3,1); t mod 8=4: (2,1); t mod 8=5: (3,1); t mod 8=6: (4,1); t mod 8=7: (4,2). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (3,8); t mod 6=1: (3,7); t mod 6=2: (2,7); t mod 6=3: (2,8); t mod 6=4: (2,7); t mod 6=5: (3,7). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (6,8) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (9,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (4,6) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (3,9) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 40 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 8 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (7,2) at t=0. I need to deliver Parcel 1 (pickup at 6,8, delivery at 9,4) and Parcel 2 (pickup at 4,6, delivery at 3,9).
First, let's check the guards' positions at t=0.
Guard P (cycle 8): t=0 -> (3,2).
Guard Q (cycle 6): t=0 -> (3,8).

My immediate goal is to pick up Parcel 1 at (6,8).
Path to (6,8):
From (7,2), I can go West to (6,2), then North to (6,8).
Let's check for obstacles/guards on this path.
(6,2) is open.
(6,3) is Gate A. Gate A is open at t mod 6 in {0,1,2}.
At t=0, Gate A is open.
At t=1, Gate A is open.
At t=2, Gate A is open.
At t=3, Gate A is closed.

Let's trace the path:
t=0: At (7,2). Move W to (6,2). Arrive t=1.
t=1: At (6,2). Move N to (6,3). Arrive t=2. Gate A is open (2 mod 6 = 2). Safe.
t=2: At (6,3). Move N to (6,4). Arrive t=3.
t=3: At (6,4). Move N to (6,5). Arrive t=4.
t=4: At (6,5). Move N to (6,6). Arrive t=5.
t=5: At (6,6). Move N to (6,7). Arrive t=6.
t=6: At (6,7). Move N to (6,8). Arrive t=7.
At (6,8) is Parcel 1. I pick it up automatically.

Wait, I need to check Guard P and Q positions along this path.
Guard P at t=0: (3,2).
Guard P at t=1: (4,2).
Guard P at t=2: (4,1).
Guard P at t=3: (3,1).
Guard P at t=4: (2,1).
Guard P at t=5: (3,1).
Guard P at t=6: (4,1).
Guard P at t=7: (4,2).
Guard P is never at column 6 or row 8 in the first few ticks. Safe.

Guard Q at t=0: (3,8).
Guard Q at t=1: (3,7).
Guard Q at t=2: (2,7).
Guard Q at t=3: (2,8).
Guard Q at t=4: (2,7).
Guard Q at t=5: (3,7).
Guard Q is never at column 6 or row 8 in the first few ticks. Safe.

So the plan to get to (6,8) is: W, N, N, N, N.
Let's verify the sequence of 5 moves.
Start: (7,2), t=0.
Move 1: W -> (6,2). Arrive t=1.
Move 2: N -> (6,3). Arrive t=2. Gate A open? Yes (2 mod 6 = 2).
Move 3: N -> (6,4). Arrive t=3.
Move 4: N -> (6,5). Arrive t=4.
Move 5: N -> (6,6). Arrive t=5.
End of plan position: (6,6).
I still need to go to (6,7) and (6,8). That's 2 more moves.
Then I need to go to (9,4) to deliver Parcel 1.
From (6,6) to (9,4):
(6,6) -> (7,6) -> (8,6) -> (9,6) -> (9,5) -> (9,4).
Let's check guards for this segment.
t=6: P at (4,1), Q at (3,7).
t=7: P at (4,2), Q at (3,8).
t=8: P at (3,2), Q at (2,7).
t=9: P at (4,2), Q at (2,8).
t=10: P at (4,1), Q at (2,7).
t=11: P at (3,1), Q at (3,7).
t=12: P at (4,2), Q at (3,8).
t=13: P at (4,1), Q at (2,7).
t=14: P at (3,1), Q at (2,8).
t=15: P at (4,2), Q at (3,7).
t=16: P at (4,1), Q at (2,7).
t=17: P at (3,1), Q at (3,8).
t=18: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=19: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=20: P at (3,2), Q at (3,7).
t=21: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=22: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=23: P at (3,1), Q at (3,7).
t=24: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=25: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=26: P at (3,1), Q at (3,8).
t=27: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=28: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=29: P at (3,1), Q at (3,7).
t=30: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=31: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=32: P at (3,2), Q at (3,7).
t=33: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=34: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=35: P at (3,1), Q at (3,8).
t=36: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=37: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).
t=38: P at (3,1), Q at (3,7).
t=39: P at (4,2), Q at (2,7).
t=40: P at (4,1), Q at (2,8).

The path (6,6)->(7,6)->(8,6)->(9,6)->(9,5)->(9,4) seems clear of guards.
However, I need to check Gate B at (7,4) if I go that way? No, I'm going via row 6.
Wait, (9,4) is the delivery spot.
Is there a wall at (9,4)? Map says:
Row 9: ..y...... -> (3,9) is y. (9,4) is '.'.
Row 4: ..#...B.x -> (9,4) is 'x'.
So (9,4) is open.

Let's re-evaluate the first plan.
Current: (7,2).
Target: (6,8) to pick up Parcel 1.
Moves: W, N, N, N, N.
t=0: (7,2) -> W -> (6,2) at t=1.
t=1: (6,2) -> N -> (6,3) at t=2. Gate A open? 2 mod 6 = 2. Yes.
t=2: (6,3) -> N -> (6,4) at t=3.
t=3: (6,4) -> N -> (6,5) at t=4.
t=4: (6,5) -> N -> (6,6) at t=5.
End of plan at (6,6).
Remaining moves to (6,8): N, N.
Then to (9,4): E, E, E, S, S.
Total moves so far: 5 + 2 + 5 = 12 moves.
Then pick up Parcel 2 at (4,6).
From (9,4) to (4,6):
(9,4) -> (4,4) -> (4,5) -> (4,6).
Path: W, W, W, W, W, N, N.
Then deliver to (3,9).
From (4,6) to (3,9):
(4,6) -> (3,6) -> (3,7) -> (3,8) -> (3,9).
Path: W, N, N, N.
Total moves: 12 + 7 + 4 = 23 moves.
This is well within 40 ticks.

Let's double check the guard positions again to ensure no collision.
Guard P cycle: 0:(3,2), 1:(4,2), 2:(4,1), 3:(3,1), 4:(2,1), 5:(3,1), 6:(4,1), 7:(4,2).
Guard Q cycle: 0:(3,8), 1:(3,7), 2:(2,7), 3:(2,8), 4:(2,7), 5:(3,7).

Plan 1: W, N, N, N, N.
t=0: Start (7,2).
Move W -> (6,2). Arrive t=1.
Guard P(1)=(4,2). Guard Q(1)=(3,7). No collision.
t=1: At (6,2).
Move N -> (6,3). Arrive t=2.
Guard P(2)=(4,1). Guard Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
Gate A at (6,3) is open (2 mod 6 = 2). OK.
t=2: At (6,3).
Move N -> (6,4). Arrive t=3.
Guard P(3)=(3,1). Guard Q(3)=(2,8). No collision.
t=3: At (6,4).
Move N -> (6,5). Arrive t=4.
Guard P(4)=(2,1). Guard Q(4)=(2,7). No collision.
t=4: At (6,5).
Move N -> (6,6). Arrive t=5.
Guard P(5)=(3,1). Guard Q(5)=(3,7). No collision.
End of Plan 1. Position (6,6).

Plan 2: N, N, E, E, E.
t=5: At (6,6).
Move N -> (6,7). Arrive t=6.
Guard P(6)=(4,1). Guard Q(6)=(3,8). No collision.
t=6: At (6,7).
Move N -> (6,8). Arrive t=7.
Guard P(7)=(4,2). Guard Q(7)=(3,7). No collision.
Parcel 1 picked up at (6,8).
t=7: At (6,8).
Move E -> (7,8). Arrive t=8.
Guard P(8)=P(0)=(3,2). Guard Q(8)=Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
t=8: At (7,8).
Move E -> (8,8). Arrive t=9.
Guard P(9)=P(1)=(4,2). Guard Q(9)=Q(3)=(2,8). No collision.
t=9: At (8,8).
Move E -> (9,8). Arrive t=10.
Guard P(10)=P(2)=(4,1). Guard Q(10)=Q(4)=(2,7). No collision.
End of Plan 2. Position (9,8).
Wait, I wanted to go to (9,4).
From (9,8) to (9,4): S, S, S, S.
Let's adjust Plan 2 to be more direct or just continue.
Actually, from (6,6) to (9,4) via (9,8) is:
(6,6)->(6,7)->(6,8)->(7,8)->(8,8)->(9,8)->(9,7)->(9,6)->(9,5)->(9,4).
That's 2 (to 6,8) + 4 (to 9,8) + 4 (to 9,4) = 10 moves.
My previous calculation was: (6,6)->(7,6)->(8,6)->(9,6)->(9,5)->(9,4). That's 5 moves.
Let's check the path (6,6)->(7,6)->(8,6)->(9,6)->(9,5)->(9,4).
t=5: At (6,6).
Move E -> (7,6). Arrive t=6.
Guard P(6)=(4,1). Guard Q(6)=(3,8). No collision.
t=6: At (7,6).
Move E -> (8,6). Arrive t=7.
Guard P(7)=(4,2). Guard Q(7)=(3,7). No collision.
t=7: At (8,6).
Move E -> (9,6). Arrive t=8.
Guard P(8)=P(0)=(3,2). Guard Q(8)=Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
t=8: At (9,6).
Move S -> (9,5). Arrive t=9.
Guard P(9)=P(1)=(4,2). Guard Q(9)=Q(3)=(2,8). No collision.
t=9: At (9,5).
Move S -> (9,4). Arrive t=10.
Guard P(10)=P(2)=(4,1). Guard Q(10)=Q(4)=(2,7). No collision.
Parcel 1 delivered at (9,4).
End of Plan 2 (if I do 5 moves).
Moves: E, E, E, S, S.
t=5: (6,6) -> E -> (7,6) at t=6.
t=6: (7,6) -> E -> (8,6) at t=7.
t=7: (8,6) -> E -> (9,6) at t=8.
t=8: (9,6) -> S -> (9,5) at t=9.
t=9: (9,5) -> S -> (9,4) at t=10.
End of Plan 2. Position (9,4).

Plan 3: W, W, W, W, W.
t=10: At (9,4).
Move W -> (8,4). Arrive t=11.
Guard P(11)=P(3)=(3,1). Guard Q(11)=Q(5)=(3,7). No collision.
t=11: At (8,4).
Move W -> (7,4). Arrive t=12.
Guard P(12)=P(4)=(2,1). Guard Q(12)=Q(0)=(3,8). No collision.
Gate B at (7,4) is open? t=12. 12 mod 6 = 0. Open set {0,1,5}. Yes, open.
t=12: At (7,4).
Move W -> (6,4). Arrive t=13.
Guard P(13)=P(5)=(3,1). Guard Q(13)=Q(1)=(3,7). No collision.
t=13: At (6,4).
Move W -> (5,4). Arrive t=14.
Guard P(14)=P(6)=(4,1). Guard Q(14)=Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
t=14: At (5,4).
Move W -> (4,4). Arrive t=15.
Guard P(15)=P(7)=(4,2). Guard Q(15)=Q(3)=(2,8). No collision.
End of Plan 3. Position (4,4).

Plan 4: N, N.
t=15: At (4,4).
Move N -> (4,5). Arrive t=16.
Guard P(16)=P(0)=(3,2). Guard Q(16)=Q(4)=(2,7). No collision.
t=16: At (4,5).
Move N -> (4,6). Arrive t=17.
Guard P(17)=P(1)=(4,2). Guard Q(17)=Q(5)=(3,7). No collision.
Parcel 2 picked up at (4,6).
End of Plan 4. Position (4,6).
Wait, I need 5 moves per plan.
Plan 4: N, N, W, W, W?
t=15: (4,4) -> N -> (4,5) at t=16.
t=16: (4,5) -> N -> (4,6) at t=17. (Pickup)
t=17: (4,6) -> W -> (3,6) at t=18.
Guard P(18)=P(2)=(4,1). Guard Q(18)=Q(0)=(3,8). No collision.
t=18: (3,6) -> W -> (2,6) at t=19.
Guard P(19)=P(3)=(3,1). Guard Q(19)=Q(1)=(3,7). No collision.
t=19: (2,6) -> W -> (1,6) at t=20.
Guard P(20)=P(4)=(2,1). Guard Q(20)=Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
End of Plan 4. Position (1,6).

Plan 5: S, S, S, S, S.
t=20: At (1,6).
Move S -> (1,5). Arrive t=21.
Guard P(21)=P(5)=(3,1). Guard Q(21)=Q(3)=(2,8). No collision.
t=21: (1,5) -> S -> (1,4). Arrive t=22.
Guard P(22)=P(6)=(4,1). Guard Q(22)=Q(4)=(2,7). No collision.
t=22: (1,4) -> S -> (1,3). Arrive t=23.
Guard P(23)=P(7)=(4,2). Guard Q(23)=Q(5)=(3,7). No collision.
t=23: (1,3) -> S -> (1,2). Arrive t=24.
Guard P(24)=P(0)=(3,2). Guard Q(24)=Q(0)=(3,8). No collision.
t=24: (1,2) -> S -> (1,1). Arrive t=25.
Guard P(25)=P(1)=(4,2). Guard Q(25)=Q(1)=(3,7). No collision.
End of Plan 5. Position (1,1).

Plan 6: E, E, E, E, E.
t=25: At (1,1).
Move E -> (2,1). Arrive t=26.
Guard P(26)=P(2)=(4,1). Guard Q(26)=Q(2)=(2,7). No collision.
t=26: (2,1) -> E -> (3,1). Arrive t=27.
Guard P(27)=P(3)=(3,1). Guard Q(27)=Q(3)=(2,8).
Wait! At t=27, Guard P is at (3,1).
If I move E from (2,1) to (3,1) at t=26->27, I arrive at (3,1) at t=27.
Guard P is at (3,1) at t=27.
So I get CAUGHT.
I cannot go through (3,1) at t=27.
Guard P cycle: 0:(3,2), 1:(4,2), 2:(4,1), 3:(3,1), 4:(2,1), 5:(3,1), 6:(4,1), 7:(4,2).
t=

THOUGHTS: I am at (7,2) at t=0. I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (6,8) and deliver it to (9,4), then pick up Parcel 2 at (4,6) and deliver it to (3,9).
First, I will move West to (6,2) and then North to (6,8) to pick up Parcel 1.
Path: (7,2) -> (6,2) -> (6,3) -> (6,4) -> (6,5) -> (6,6).
Moves: W, N, N, N, N.

Then to (9,4): E, E, E, S, S.

Next, go to (9,4) to deliver Parcel 1.
From (9,8) to (9,4): S, S, S, S.
Combine into next 5 moves: S, S, S, S, S.
t=10: At (9,8). Move S to (9,7). Arrive t=11.

t=12: At (9,6). Move S to (9,5). Arrive t=13.
t=13: At (9,5). Move S to (9,4). Arrive t=14. Parcel 1 delivered.
t=14: At (9,4). Move S to (9,3). Arrive t=15.
End of plan at (9,3).
Next, go to (4,6) to pick up Parcel 2.
From (9,3) to (4,6): W, W, W, W, W, N, N.
Combine into next 5 moves: W, W, W, W, W.
t=15: At (9,3). Move W to (8,3). Arrive t=16.
t=16: At (8,3). Move W to (7,3). Arrive t=17.
t=17: At (7,3). Move W to (6,3). Arrive t=18.
t=18: At (6,3). Move W to (5,3). Arrive t=19.
t=19: At (5,3). Move W to (4,3). Arrive t=20.
End of plan at (4,3).
Next, go to (4,6) to pick up Parcel 2.
From (4,3) to (4,6): N, N, N.
Combine into next 5 moves: N, N, N, W, W.
t=20: At (4,3). Move N to (4,4). Arrive t=21.
t=21: At (4,4). Move N to (4,5). Arrive t=22.
t=22: At (4,5). Move N to (4,6). Arrive t=23. Parcel 2 picked up.
t=23: At (4,6). Move W to (3,6). Arrive t=24.

End of plan at (2,6).
Next, go to (3,9) to deliver Parcel 2.

t=25: At (2,6). Move E to (3,6). Arrive t=26.
t=26: At (3,6). Move N to (3,7). Arrive t=27.
t=27: At (3,7). Move N to (3,8). Arrive t=28.
t=28: At (3,8). Move N to (3,9). Arrive t=29. Parcel 2 delivered.

End of plan at (3,8).
All parcels delivered.
